title: Populate shared followup touched_files from tester and docs bead dependencies
description:
The _populate_shared_followup_touched_files method in scheduler.py only aggregates touched_files and changed_files from developer bead dependencies. When a shared review bead depends on tester or documentation beads that completed with non-empty touched_files, those files are ignored. When tester/docs beads report empty touched_files (as B-9c9181df did), the review bead ends up with empty touched_files and the review guardrail blocks it as unverifiable.

Fix: extend _populate_shared_followup_touched_files to aggregate touched_files and changed_files from ALL done bead dependencies, not just developer beads. The existing deduplication logic (files appearing in multiple beads' handoff summaries are deduplicated) should be preserved.

The guard condition 'skip if fewer than two done developer dependencies' should be relaxed to 'skip if no done dependencies with non-empty touched_files exist'. The goal is to ensure the shared review bead always has a populated file scope by the time it is dispatched.

File to modify: src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py — _populate_shared_followup_touched_files method.
acceptance_criteria: _populate_shared_followup_touched_files aggregates touched_files from done dependencies of any agent type (developer, tester, documentation)
Deduplication of file paths across multiple dependencies is preserved
Method is a no-op when no done dependencies have non-empty touched_files
Existing tests for the method still pass
New test covers the case where a tester bead dependency contributes touched_files to a shared review bead

handoff_summary.completed: Updated `_populate_shared_followup_touched_files` in `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` to aggregate `touched_files` and `changed_files` from all done dependencies, preserving deduplication and only no-oping when no done dependency reports non-empty `touched_files`. Updated `tests/test_orchestrator.py` to reflect the relaxed guard and cover tester dependency contribution plus the no-touched-files no-op case. Verified syntax with `python -m py_compile src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py tests/test_orchestrator.py`.
handoff_summary.remaining: Automated test execution was not run per developer guardrails. Tester should run the scheduler/orchestrator test coverage that exercises shared followup touched-file aggregation.
handoff_summary.risks: Behavior is syntax-checked but not test-validated in this pass. The acceptance criteria about existing tests passing remains for the tester agent to confirm.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run the relevant orchestrator test coverage to confirm the updated aggregation behavior and the new tester-dependency regression case.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low merge risk if adjacent scheduler followup logic changes concurrently; downstream agents should preserve the relaxed guard semantics and the new tester-dependency coverage.

conflict_risks: Low merge risk if adjacent scheduler followup logic changes concurrently; downstream agents should preserve the relaxed guard semantics and the new tester-dependency coverage.

execution_history.summary: Extended shared followup scope population to read done dependencies of any agent type and added coverage for single-dependency, tester-contributed, and no-touched-files cases.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
